# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later

"""Get darkmode arguments to pass to Qt.

Overview of blink setting names based on the Qt version:

Qt 5.10 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

First implementation, called "high contrast mode".

- highContrastMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness)
- highContrastGrayscale (bool)
- highContrastContrast (float)
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone)

Qt 5.11, 5.12, 5.13 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------------------

New "smart" image policy.

- Mode/Grayscale/Contrast as above
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart [new!])

Qt 5.14 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

Renamed to "darkMode".

- darkMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness/
            kInvertLightnessLAB [new!])
- darkModeGrayscale (bool)
- darkModeContrast (float)
- darkModeImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart)
- darkModePagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterByBackground) [new!]
- darkModeTextBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeBackgroundBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]
- darkModeImageGrayscale (float) [new!]

Qt 5.15.0 and 5.15.1 (UNSUPPORTED)
----------------------------------

"darkMode" split into "darkModeEnabled" and "darkModeInversionAlgorithm".

- darkModeEnabled (bool) [new!]
- darkModeInversionAlgorithm (kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/
                                kInvertLightness/kInvertLightnessLAB)
- Rest (except darkMode) as above.
- NOTE: smart image policy is broken with Qt 5.15.0!

Qt 5.15.2
---------

Prefix changed to "forceDarkMode".

- As with Qt 5.15.0 / .1, but with "forceDarkMode" as prefix.

Qt 5.15.3
---------

Settings split to new --dark-mode-settings switch:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2390588

- Everything except forceDarkModeEnabled goes to the other switch.
- Algorithm uses a different enum with kOff gone.
- No "forceDarkMode" prefix anymore.

Removed DarkModePagePolicy:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2323441

"prefers color scheme dark" changed enum values:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2232922

- Now needs to be 0 for dark and 1 for light
  (before: 0 no preference / 1 dark / 2 light)

Qt 6.2
------

No significant changes over 5.15.3

Qt 6.3
------

- New IncreaseTextContrast:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2893236

Qt 6.4
------

- Renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold

  "Correct brightness threshold of darkmode color classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100

  "Rename text_classifier to foreground_classifier"
  https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3226389
"""

import os
import copy
import enum
import dataclasses
import collections
from typing import (Any, Iterator, Mapping, MutableMapping, Optional, Set, Tuple, Union,
                    Sequence, List)

from qutebrowser.config import config
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, utils, log, version


_BLINK_SETTINGS = 'blink-settings'


class Variant(enum.Enum):

    """A dark mode variant."""

    qt_515_2 = enum.auto()
    qt_515_3 = enum.auto()
    qt_63 = enum.auto()
    qt_64 = enum.auto()


# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.algorithm setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeInversionAlgorithm enum values.
_ALGORITHMS = {
    # 0: kOff (not exposed)
    # 1: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 2,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 3,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 4,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}
# Qt >= 5.15.3, based on dark_mode_settings.h
_ALGORITHMS_NEW = {
    # 0: kSimpleInvertForTesting (not exposed)
    'brightness-rgb': 1,  # kInvertBrightness
    'lightness-hsl': 2,  # kInvertLightness
    'lightness-cielab': 3,  # kInvertLightnessLAB
}

# Mapping from a colors.webpage.darkmode.policy.images setting value to
# Chromium's DarkModeImagePolicy enum values.
# Values line up with dark_mode_settings.h for 5.15.3+.
_IMAGE_POLICIES = {
    'always': 0,  # kFilterAll
    'never': 1,  # kFilterNone
    'smart': 2,  # kFilterSmart

[stdout]

---------------------

Qt 5.11, 5.12, 5.13 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------------------

- Mode/Grayscale/Contrast as above
- highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart [new!])

Qt 5.14 (UNSUPPORTED)
---------------------

Renamed to "darkMode".

- darkMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness/
            kInvertLightnessLAB [new!])
- darkModeGrayscale (bool)
- darkModeContrast (float)
- darkModeImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart)
- darkModePagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterByBackground) [new!]

- darkModeBackgroundBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]

Qt 5.15.0 and 5.15.1 (UNSUPPORTED)

                                kInvertLightness/kInvertLightnessLAB)

Qt 5.15.2
---------

Prefix changed to "forceDarkMode".

Qt 5.15.3
---------

Settings split to new --dark-mode-settings switch:
https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2390588

  (before: 0 no preference / 1 dark / 2 light)

------

Qt 6.4
------

results.content:
if switch_names is not None:
            self._switch_names = switch_names
        else:
            self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}

    def prefixed_settings(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, _Setting]]:
        """Get all "prepared" settings.

        Yields tuples which contain the Chromium setting key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or
        'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.
        """
        for setting in self._settings:
            switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])
            yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)

    def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.

        NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the
        same (immutable) tuple.
        """
        new = copy.copy(self)
        setattr(new, attr, value)
        return new

    def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting."""
        new = copy.copy(self)
        new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access
        return new

    def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.

        If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.
        """
        new = copy.deepcopy(self)

        for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access
            if setting.option == option:
                setting.chromium_key = chromium_key
                return new

        raise ValueError(f"Setting {option} not found in {self}")


# Our defaults for policy.images are different from Chromium's, so we mark it as
# mandatory setting.

_DEFINITIONS: MutableMapping[Variant, _Definition] = {
    Variant.qt_515_2: _Definition(
        _Setting('enabled', 'Enabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'Contrast'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'Grayscale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('policy.page', 'PagePolicy', _PAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayscale'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='forceDarkMode',
    ),

    Variant.qt_515_3: _Definition(
        # Different switch for settings
        _Setting('enabled', 'forceDarkModeEnabled', _BOOLS),
        _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS_NEW),

        _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),
        _Setting('contrast', 'ContrastPercent'),
        _Setting('grayscale.all', 'IsGrayScale', _BOOLS),

        _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),
        _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayScalePercent'),

        mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},
        prefix='',
        switch_names={'enabled': _BLINK_SETTINGS, None: 'dark-mode-settings'},
    ),
}
_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_515_3].copy_add_setting(

        *,

) -> Mapping[str, Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]]:
    """Get necessary blink settings to configure dark mode for QtWebEngine.

    Args:
       Existing '--blink-settings=...' flags, if any.

    Returns:
        A dict which maps Chromium switch names (blink-settings or dark-mode-settings)
        to a sequence of tuples, each tuple being a key/value pair to pass to that
        setting.
    """
    variant = _variant(versions)
    log.init.debug(f"Darkmode variant: {variant.name}")

    result: Mapping[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = collections.defaultdict(list)

    blink_settings_flag = f'--{_BLINK_SETTINGS}='
    for flag in special_flags:
        if flag.startswith(blink_settings_flag):
            for pair in flag[len(blink_settings_flag):].split(','):
                key, val = pair.split('=', maxsplit=1)
                result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((key, val))

    preferred_color_scheme_key = config.instance.get(
        "colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme", fallback=False)
    preferred_color_scheme_defs = _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]
    if preferred_color_scheme_key in preferred_color_scheme_defs:
        value = preferred_color_scheme_defs[preferred_color_scheme_key]
        result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append(("preferredColorScheme", value))

    if not config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled:
        return result

    definition = _DEFINITIONS[variant]

    for switch_name, setting in definition.prefixed_settings():
        # To avoid blowing up the commandline length, we only pass modified
        # settings to Chromium, as our defaults line up with Chromium's.
        # However, we always pass enabled/algorithm to make sure dark mode gets
        # actually turned on.
        value = config.instance.get(
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.' + setting.option,
            fallback=setting.option in definition.mandatory)
        if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):
            continue

        result[switch_name].append(setting.chromium_tuple(value))

    return result

    def prefixed_settings(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, _Setting]]:
        """Get all "prepared" settings.

        Yields tuples which contain the Chromium setting key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or
        'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.
        """
        for setting in self._settings:
            switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])
            yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)

    def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.

        NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the
        same (immutable) tuple.
        """
        new = copy.copy(self)
        setattr(new, attr, value)
        return new

    def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting."""
        new = copy.copy(self)
        new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access
        return new

    def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':
        """Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.

        If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.
        """
        new = copy.deepcopy(self)

        for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access
            if setting.option == option:
                setting.chromium_key = chromium_key
                return new

        raise ValueError(f"Setting {option} not found in {self}")

def _variant(versions: version.WebEngineVersions) -> Variant:
    """Get the dark mode variant based on the underlying Qt version."""
    env_var = os.environ.get('QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT')
    if env_var is not None:
        try:
            return Variant[env_var]
        except KeyError:
            log.init.warning(f"Ignoring invalid QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT={env_var}")

    if versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 4):
        return Variant.qt_64
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 3):
        return Variant.qt_63
    elif (versions.webengine == utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2) and
            versions.chromium_major == 87):
        # WORKAROUND for Gentoo packaging something newer as 5.15.2...
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 3):
        return Variant.qt_515_3
    elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2):
        return Variant.qt_515_2
    raise utils.Unreachable(versions.webengine)


def settings(
        *,
        versions: version.WebEngineVersions,
        special_flags: Sequence[str],
) -> Mapping[str, Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]]:
    """Get necessary blink settings to configure dark mode for QtWebEngine.

    Args:
       Existing '--blink-settings=...' flags, if any.

    Returns:
        A dict which maps Chromium switch names (blink-settings or dark-mode-settings)
        to a sequence of tuples, each tuple being a key/value pair to pass to that
        setting.
    """
    variant = _variant(versions)
    log.init.debug(f"Darkmode variant: {variant.name}")

    result: Mapping[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = collections.defaultdict(list)

    blink_settings_flag = f'--{_BLINK_SETTINGS}='
    for flag in special_flags:
        if flag.startswith(blink_settings_flag):
            for pair in flag[len(blink_settings_flag):].split(','):
                key, val = pair.split('=', maxsplit=1)
                result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((key, val))

    preferred_color_scheme_key = config.instance.get(
        "colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme", fallback=False)
    preferred_color_scheme_defs = _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]
    if preferred_color_scheme_key in preferred_color_scheme_defs:
        value = preferred_color_scheme_defs[preferred_color_scheme_key]
        result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append(("preferredColorScheme", value))

    if not config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled:
        return result

    definition = _DEFINITIONS[variant]

    for switch_name, setting in definition.prefixed_settings():
        # To avoid blowing up the commandline length, we only pass modified
        # settings to Chromium, as our defaults line up with Chromium's.
        # However, we always pass enabled/algorithm to make sure dark mode gets
        # actually turned on.
        value = config.instance.get(
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.' + setting.option,
            fallback=setting.option in definition.mandatory)
        if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):
            continue

        result[switch_name].append(setting.chromium_tuple(value))

    return result

steps.message:
Now I understand the issue! The current code (HEAD at 2023-09-21) is missing the Qt 6.4 variant support that was added on 2023-09-22 in commit 50efac08f. The bug is that Qt 6.4 renamed the Chromium setting from `TextBrightnessThreshold` to `ForegroundBrightnessThreshold`, but the current code doesn't account for this.

Let me create a regression test that demonstrates this bug:

arguments.content:
"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys
import os

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """Initialize the config system for testing."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)

    # Initialize configdata
    configdata.init(None)

    # Create a fresh config instance
    config_obj = config.Config()
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', config_obj)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', config_obj)


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting should still be 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected TextBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('TextBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null --timeout=60 /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

arguments.old_string:
"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys
import os

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """Initialize the config system for testing."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)

    # Initialize configdata
    configdata.init(None)

    # Create a fresh config instance
    config_obj = config.Config()
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', config_obj)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', config_obj)

import pytest

sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils

from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)

    configdata.init(None)

    config_obj = config.Config()
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', config_obj)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', config_obj)


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

raw_arguments.old_string:
def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )

Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

import pytest
import sys

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils

from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)

def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(config_stub, init_configdata):

    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...

    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (

    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null --timeout=60 /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def init_configdata():
    """Initialize configdata if needed."""
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """

    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def init_configdata():
    """Initialize configdata if needed."""
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63():
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting should still be 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected TextBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('TextBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null --timeout=60 /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def init_configdata():
    """Initialize configdata if needed."""
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting should still be 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected TextBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('TextBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null --timeout=60 /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later


import logging

import pytest

from qutebrowser.config import configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def gentoo_versions():
    return version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2),
        chromium='87.0.4280.144',
        source='faked',
    )


@pytest.mark.parametrize('value, webengine_version, expected', [
    # Auto
    ("auto", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),  # QTBUG-89753
    ("auto", "5.15.3", []),
    ("auto", "6.2.0", []),

    # Unset
    (None, "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),  # QTBUG-89753
    (None, "5.15.3", []),
    (None, "6.2.0", []),

    # Dark
    ("dark", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    ("dark", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),
    ("dark", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),

    # Light
    ("light", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),
    ("light", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    ("light", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
])
def test_colorscheme(config_stub, value, webengine_version, expected):
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt(webengine_version)
    if value is not None:
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = value

    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected


def test_colorscheme_gentoo_workaround(config_stub, gentoo_versions):
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = "dark"
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=gentoo_versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]


@pytest.mark.parametrize('settings, expected', [
    # Disabled
    ({}, [('preferredColorScheme', '2')]),

    # Enabled without customization
    (
        {'enabled': True},
        [
            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
        ]
    ),

    # Algorithm
    (
        {'enabled': True, 'algorithm': 'brightness-rgb'},
        [
            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
            ('forceDarkModeInversionAlgorithm', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
        ],
    ),
])
def test_basics(config_stub, settings, expected):
    for k, v in settings.items():
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.' + k, v)

    # Using Qt 5.15.2 because it has the least special cases.
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('5.15.2')
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected

import logging

import pytest

from qutebrowser.config import configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def gentoo_versions():
    return version.WebEngineVersions(
        webengine=utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2),
        chromium='87.0.4280.144',
        source='faked',
    )


@pytest.mark.parametrize('value, webengine_version, expected', [
    # Auto
    ("auto", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),  # QTBUG-89753
    ("auto", "5.15.3", []),
    ("auto", "6.2.0", []),

    # Unset
    (None, "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),  # QTBUG-89753
    (None, "5.15.3", []),
    (None, "6.2.0", []),

    # Dark
    ("dark", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    ("dark", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),
    ("dark", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),

    # Light
    ("light", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),
    ("light", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    ("light", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
])
def test_colorscheme(config_stub, value, webengine_version, expected):
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt(webengine_version)
    if value is not None:
        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = value

    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected


def test_colorscheme_gentoo_workaround(config_stub, gentoo_versions):
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = "dark"
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=gentoo_versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]


@pytest.mark.parametrize('settings, expected', [
    # Disabled
    ({}, [('preferredColorScheme', '2')]),

    # Enabled without customization
    (
        {'enabled': True},
        [
            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
        ]
    ),

    # Algorithm
    (
        {'enabled': True, 'algorithm': 'brightness-rgb'},
        [
            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
            ('forceDarkModeInversionAlgorithm', '2'),
            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
        ],
    ),
])
def test_basics(config_stub, settings, expected):
    for k, v in settings.items():
        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.' + k, v)

    # Using Qt 5.15.2 because it has the least special cases.
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('5.15.2')
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected

    38→    (None, "5.15.3", []),
    39→    (None, "6.2.0", []),
    40→
    41→    # Dark
    42→    ("dark", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    43→    ("dark", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),
    44→    ("dark", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]),
    45→
    46→    # Light
    47→    ("light", "5.15.2", [("preferredColorScheme", "2")]),
    48→    ("light", "5.15.3", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    49→    ("light", "6.2.0", [("preferredColorScheme", "1")]),
    50→])
    51→def test_colorscheme(config_stub, value, webengine_version, expected):
    52→    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt(webengine_version)
    53→    if value is not None:
    54→        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = value
    55→
    56→    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    57→    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected
    58→
    59→
    60→def test_colorscheme_gentoo_workaround(config_stub, gentoo_versions):
    61→    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = "dark"
    62→    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=gentoo_versions, special_flags=[])
    63→    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == [("preferredColorScheme", "0")]
    64→
    65→
    66→@pytest.mark.parametrize('settings, expected', [
    67→    # Disabled
    68→    ({}, [('preferredColorScheme', '2')]),
    69→
    70→    # Enabled without customization
    71→    (
    72→        {'enabled': True},
    73→        [
    74→            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
    75→            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
    76→            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
    77→        ]
    78→    ),
    79→
    80→    # Algorithm
    81→    (
    82→        {'enabled': True, 'algorithm': 'brightness-rgb'},
    83→        [
    84→            ('preferredColorScheme', '2'),
    85→            ('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true'),
    86→            ('forceDarkModeInversionAlgorithm', '2'),
    87→            ('forceDarkModeImagePolicy', '2'),
    88→        ],
    89→    ),
    90→])
    91→def test_basics(config_stub, settings, expected):
    92→    for k, v in settings.items():
    93→        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.' + k, v)
    94→
    95→    # Using Qt 5.15.2 because it has the least special cases.
    96→    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('5.15.2')
    97→    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])
    98→    assert darkmode_settings['blink-settings'] == expected
    99→

arguments.content:
"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import sys
import os

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')
os.chdir('/app')

import pytest
from unittest import mock

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch):
    """Initialize config for darkmode testing."""
    # Initialize configdata if needed
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()

    # Create a mock config that allows setting values
    mock_config = mock.MagicMock()
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = False
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET

    # Default values for dark mode settings
    mock_config.instance.get = mock.MagicMock(side_effect=lambda key, fallback=False: (
        fallback if key not in {
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled',
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text',
            'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme',
        } else (
            mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled if 'enabled' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text if 'threshold.text' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme
        )
    ))

    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', mock_config)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', mock_config.val)

    yield mock_config


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Mock the config.instance.get to return our values
    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):
        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Mock the config.instance.get to return our values
    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):
        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting should still be 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected TextBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('TextBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because

'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

for Qt 6.4.

import sys
import os

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')
os.chdir('/app')

import pytest
from unittest import mock

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)

    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch):
    """Initialize config for darkmode testing."""
    # Initialize configdata if needed
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()

    # Create a mock config that allows setting values
    mock_config = mock.MagicMock()
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = False
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET

    # Default values for dark mode settings
    mock_config.instance.get = mock.MagicMock(side_effect=lambda key, fallback=False: (
        fallback if key not in {
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled',
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text',
            'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme',
        } else (
            mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled if 'enabled' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text if 'threshold.text' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme
        )
    ))

    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', mock_config)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', mock_config.val)

    yield mock_config


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.

    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions

    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (

    )

def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():

"""
Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import pytest
import sys

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils

from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture
def init_configdata():
    """Initialize configdata if needed."""
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {settings_dict.keys()}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63(config_stub, init_configdata):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)

    # Get the dark mode settings

    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64(init_configdata):

import sys
import os

sys.path.insert(0, '/app')
os.chdir('/app')

import pytest
from unittest import mock

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata

from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch):
    """Initialize config for darkmode testing."""
    # Initialize configdata if needed
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()

    # Create a mock config that allows setting values
    mock_config = mock.MagicMock()
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = False
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET

    # Default values for dark mode settings
    mock_config.instance.get = mock.MagicMock(side_effect=lambda key, fallback=False: (
        fallback if key not in {
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled',
            'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text',
            'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme',
        } else (
            mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled if 'enabled' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text if 'threshold.text' in key
            else mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme
        )
    ))

    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', mock_config)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', mock_config.val)

    yield mock_config


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.

        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':

        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.

    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (

    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3

        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"

def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """

def init_config(monkeypatch):
    """Initialize config for darkmode testing."""
    # Initialize configdata if needed

    # Create a mock config that allows setting values

    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = False
    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET

def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Mock the config.instance.get to return our values
    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):
        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )

Regression test for dark mode text threshold setting on Qt 6.4.

Bug: When using Qt 6.4, the dark mode text brightness threshold setting
(colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text) has no effect. This is because
Qt 6.4's Chromium renamed the setting from 'TextBrightnessThreshold' to
'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but qutebrowser still uses the old name.

This test verifies that the setting is correctly mapped to the new name
for Qt 6.4.
"""

import sys
import os

# Add the app directory to the path so we can import qutebrowser modules
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')
os.chdir('/app')

import pytest
from unittest import mock

from qutebrowser.config import config, configdata
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version, utils
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode
from qutebrowser.misc import objects


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_backend(monkeypatch):
    """Patch the backend to QtWebEngine."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def init_config(monkeypatch):
    """Initialize config for darkmode testing."""
    # Initialize configdata if needed
    if configdata.DATA is None:
        configdata.init()

    # Create a real config object
    config_inst = config.Config()

    # Create mock for config.instance
    mock_instance = mock.MagicMock()

    def make_get_func(initial_values):
        def getter(key, fallback=False):
            return initial_values.get(key, fallback)
        return getter

    # Store initial values for the mock
    initial_values = {
        'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled': False,
        'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text': usertypes.UNSET,
        'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme': usertypes.UNSET,
    }

    mock_instance.get = make_get_func(initial_values)

    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'instance', mock_instance)
    monkeypatch.setattr(config, 'val', config_inst)

    yield initial_values, mock_instance


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt64(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.4.

    On Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that qutebrowser correctly uses the new name.

    Regression test for: https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/...
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Mock the config.instance.get to return our values
    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):
        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':
            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.4 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.4, the setting should be 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', not 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected ForegroundBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the old setting name is NOT used
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.4 should NOT use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_text_threshold_qt63(init_config):
    """
    Test that text threshold setting uses correct Chromium setting name for Qt 6.3.

    On Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is still called TextBrightnessThreshold.
    This test verifies that the fix for Qt 6.4 doesn't break Qt 6.3.
    """
    # Setup: enable dark mode and set text threshold
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True
    init_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text = 100

    # Mock the config.instance.get to return our values
    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):
        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':
            return True
        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':
            return 100

            return usertypes.UNSET
        else:
            return fallback

    init_config.instance.get = mock_get

    # Use Qt 6.3 versions
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3.0')

    # Get the dark mode settings
    darkmode_settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])

    # The text threshold should be in the dark-mode-settings
    dark_mode_settings_list = darkmode_settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])

    # Convert to a dict for easier assertions
    settings_dict = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)

    # For Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting should still be 'TextBrightnessThreshold'
    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should use 'TextBrightnessThreshold' setting, but got: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )
    assert settings_dict['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (
        f"Expected TextBrightnessThreshold value to be '100', got: {settings_dict.get('TextBrightnessThreshold')}"
    )

    # Ensure the new setting name is NOT used on Qt 6.3
    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in settings_dict, (
        f"Qt 6.3 should NOT use 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', but it's present in: {list(settings_dict.keys())}"
    )


def test_dark_mode_variant_detection_qt64():
    """
    Test that the variant detection correctly identifies Qt 6.4.
    """
    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4.0')
    variant = darkmode._variant(versions)

    # Qt 6.4 should have its own variant (if the fix is applied)
    # If the fix is not applied, it will fall through to qt_63
    expected_variant_name = 'qt_64'

    assert variant.name == expected_variant_name, (
        f"Expected variant name '{expected_variant_name}' for Qt 6.4, got '{variant.name}'. "
        f"This indicates Qt 6.4 support is missing."
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Run with: python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null /workspace/regression_test.py
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])
